title: Other vocational and industrial trainers
description: Design or conduct work-related training and development programs to improve individual skills or organizational performance. May analyze organizational training needs or evaluate training effectiveness. Review, evaluate, and analyze work environments and design programs and procedures to control, eliminate, and prevent disease or injury caused by chemical, physical, and biological agents or ergonomic factors. May conduct inspections and enforce adherence to laws and regulations governing the health and safety of individuals. May be employed in the public or private sector. Provide and manage health education programs that help individuals, families, and their communities maximize and maintain healthy lifestyles. Use data to identify community needs prior to planning, implementing, monitoring, and evaluating programs designed to encourage healthy lifestyles, policies, and environments. May link health systems, health providers, insurers, and patients to address individual and population health needs. May serve as resource to assist individuals, other health professionals, or the community, and may administer fiscal resources for health education programs.

tasks.task: Keep up with developments in area of expertise by reading current journals, books, or magazine articles.

Design, plan, organize, or direct orientation and training programs for employees or customers, including developing or implementing training programs related to efficiency, recycling, or other issues with environmental impacts.

Present information with a variety of instructional techniques or formats, such as role playing, simulations, team exercises, group discussions, videos, or lectures, and conduct safety training or education programs while demonstrating the use of safety equipment.

Evaluate modes of training delivery, such as in-person or virtual, to optimize training effectiveness, costs, or environmental impacts; monitor and assess training needs through surveys, interviews, and consultations with relevant stakeholders; and evaluate training materials prepared by instructors.

Monitor training costs and prepare budget reports to justify expenditures, while organizing and developing training procedure manuals, guides, or course materials such as handouts or visual materials.
Supervise, evaluate, or refer instructors to skill development classes, select and assign instructors to conduct training, and coordinate recruitment and placement of training program participants.

Negotiate contracts with clients for desired training outcomes, fees, or expenses, and attend meetings or seminars to obtain information for use in training programs or to inform management of training program status.

Schedule classes based on availability of classrooms, equipment, or instructors, and document activities and record information, such as the numbers of applications completed, presentations conducted, and persons assisted.
Monitor, evaluate, or record training activities or program effectiveness and develop alternative training methods if expected improvements are not seen.

Conduct health-related inspections, investigations, and audits at hazardous sites to ensure compliance with public health legislation and regulations, while inspecting specified areas for fire prevention or safety equipment.
Collect samples of hazardous materials or arrange for their collection, and analyze incident data to identify trends in injuries, illnesses, accidents, or other hazards.
Develop, prepare, and present health education materials and programs, including training workshops, educational materials for community agencies, and health promotion programs.
Develop operational plans, policies, and health education objectives, and maintain databases and resources for health education programs.

Collaborate with health specialists and civic groups to determine community health needs and develop goals for meeting those needs, as well as provide guidance on health education assessments and program development.
Supervise professional and technical staff in implementing health programs and provide program information to the public through various channels.

Investigate the adequacy of workplace conditions that could affect employee health, comfort, or performance, and recommend measures to help protect workers from potentially hazardous work methods, processes, or materials.
Develop or maintain hygiene and emergency response programs, and document findings and write reports.
Maintain inventories of hazardous materials or wastes, prepare hazardous sample for transportation, and perform laboratory analyses to assess the presence and risk of hazardous materials.
Refer trainees to organizations offering job placement assistance or appropriate social services agencies, if deemed necessary.
